problem: Count the complex numbers z with z^9 equal to its own conjugate.
A positive integer n has lcm(n,45)=10710 and gcd(n,15)=1. What is the smallest such n?
The 16th roots of unity and the 10th roots of unity share how many common values?
Add the differences of consecutive cubes 2³-1³, 4³-3³, ..., 36³-35³.
Take the three smallest integers with exactly 6 divisors as a box's dimensions. What is the square of its space diagonal?
Among triangular numbers below 4844, how many are multiples of 3?
How many right triangles with integer side lengths have a hypotenuse of at most 18?
With 8 glasses, 7 full and one at 3/4, the equalizing pour from each full glass is m/n irreducible. What is m+n?
Write the continued fraction with 5 levels of 5 (i.e. 5+1/(5+1/(...))) as a reduced fraction m/n; find m+n.
Of the divisors of 470, what do the even ones add up to?
